medications.indication_rationale: First-line for functional constipation; source used 2 sachets daily until regular then titrate to 1 daily. Needs adequate fluid intake.
medications.dose_route_frequency: 2 sachets daily until regular, then 1 daily maintenance (as per source note)

medications.drug_name: micro-enema (e.g. sodium citrate) for acute rectal loading
medications.indication_rationale: Relieves acute distal rectal loading; source gave a micro-enema in person. Self-administered enema is optional if the patient is comfortable.
medications.dose_route_frequency: single rectal micro-enema PRN (as per source note)

medications.drug_name: stimulant laxative escalation before excluding obstruction
medications.indication_rationale: Do not escalate stimulant laxatives while mechanical obstruction is a possibility (cramping) — could worsen a true obstruction. Exclude obstruction first.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Sit on the toilet for ~5 minutes about 20 minutes after breakfast; use a footstool to raise the knees above the hips.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Uses the gastrocolic reflex and optimises the anorectal angle for strain-free defecation.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Increase fluids (aim ~2L/day) and dietary fibre gradually.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Osmotic laxatives rely on available water; fibre/fluid support regular soft stool.

patient_education_points.point: This is functional constipation with backed-up stool — not a blockage, since you're still passing wind and not vomiting. The macrogol powder is a gentle water-based softener (not a harsh stimulant) and works over 24–48 hours; drink plenty of water with it.
Build a routine: toilet ~20 minutes after breakfast, use a footstool, and don't strain. Keep fluids up.

Go to ED if you stop passing wind completely, start vomiting (especially green/foul), or the pain becomes severe, constant and your abdomen goes rigid. See your GP for any rectal bleeding, weight loss, or if this becomes a persistent new change — and for bowel-cancer screening if due.

follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Response to macrogol; establish maintenance; arrange bowel-cancer screening if due; DRE/abdo exam if not settling.
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: No flatus, vomiting, severe/rigid abdomen — ED; rectal bleeding or weight loss — GP.

must_include_items: Exclude mechanical obstruction (flatus, vomiting, rigid abdomen) before laxatives
Osmotic laxative (macrogol) with adequate fluid + toileting behaviour/footstool
Alarm-feature screen + arrange bowel-cancer screening if due
acceptable_alternatives: Micro-enema for acute rectal loading; lactulose as alternative osmotic agent
errors_of_commission: Escalating stimulant laxatives without excluding obstruction
Treating as an emergency when obstruction is excluded
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: Pass requires an obstruction screen, osmotic-laxative-led management with behaviour, and alarm-feature screening.
